items.title: Sigmoid shape and saturation zones
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  negInput["x very negative"]
  midInput["x near 0"]
  posInput["x very positive"]
  lowOut["sigma(x) -> 0 lower saturation"]
  midOut["sigma(x) ~ 0.5 max slope"]
  highOut["sigma(x) -> 1 upper saturation"]
  negInput --> lowOut
  midInput --> midOut
  posInput --> highOut
items.caption: Extreme inputs land on the flat tails where σ′(x)≈0; the steepest learning happens near x=0.

items.title: Backprop through sigmoid hidden layers
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  inputNode["input features"]
  hidden1["hidden layer 1 sigma(x1)"]
  hidden2["hidden layer 2 sigma(x2)"]
  hidden3["hidden layer 3 sigma(x3)"]
  lossNode["loss L"]
  gradProd["gradient product: sigma'(x1) * sigma'(x2) * sigma'(x3)"]
  earlyW["early weights get shrunk signal"]
  inputNode --> hidden1
  hidden1 --> hidden2
  hidden2 --> hidden3
  hidden3 --> lossNode
  lossNode --> gradProd
  gradProd --> earlyW
items.caption: Each saturated sigmoid layer multiplies a factor ≤0.25 into the chain rule — the vanishing-gradient mechanism.

items.title: Where sigmoid still fits in modern nets
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  featuresNode["input features"]
  reluHidden["deep hidden layers: ReLU"]
  linearOut["linear logit z"]
  sigOut["sigma(z) probability"]
  gateNode["RNN/LSTM gates: sigma"]
  featuresNode --> reluHidden
  reluHidden --> linearOut
  linearOut --> sigOut
  featuresNode --> gateNode
items.caption: Typical pattern: ReLU in deep hidden stacks; sigmoid on binary outputs and gating where (0,1) bounds are required.
